user_prompt: implement `test_hapticfeedback`, use BtnA, BtnB, and BtnC, to fire off haptic waveforms

but do so by calling into `lib\HapticsWrapper\HapticsWrapper.cpp`, which you also need to implement. The point is that the GUI calls will call into `HapticsWrapper.cpp` without having to understand what is actually being done, just a `haptic_play_click()` call
so make it "click", "buzz" (for incoming connections), and something else that might be useful, maybe something for indicating a long task is finished
agent_reply: Implemented the haptics wrapper and the interactive test.

Implemented them in [HapticsWrapper.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/HapticsWrapper/HapticsWrapper.cpp:1) using `M5.Power.setVibration(...)` with short async FreeRTOS playback, so GUI code can call `haptic_play_click()` without knowing the waveform details.
Verification: `pio run -e thefly_sec1` passes. The base `thefly` env still fails because `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL` is not defined there, before it reaches this code.
